_comment: This is a REFERENCE EXAMPLE of a .mcp.json file.
OFFICIAL FORMAT: .mcp.json is the official Claude Code project-scoped MCP config file.
Place it at the root of your project (same level as .claude/).
It is safe to commit to git AS LONG AS it contains no secrets.
SECRETS: Never put API keys, passwords, or tokens directly in this file.
Set actual secret values in your shell environment or a .env file that is gitignored.
- You need to give Claude access to a tool or data source that Claude Code
does not natively support (e.g. a JIRA instance, a custom knowledge base,
a specific database, a vector search service).
- The integration is reusable across sessions and worth the setup cost.
- For simple read access to files already in the project — use Read/Grep/Glob.
- For running build commands — use Bash.
- For one-off lookups — it is faster to ask Claude to use WebFetch.
- When the overhead of maintaining an MCP server exceeds the benefit.
Remove the _comment key and unused server entries before using this file.

mcpServers._comment_filesystem: Remove this entry — it is documentation only.

project-docs._when_to_use: Useful when you have documentation outside the project root that Claude should be able to read — e.g. a shared specs folder, a wiki export, or generated docs in a separate directory.

postgres._when_to_use: Gives Claude read access to a database for schema inspection and query analysis. Useful for the technical-analyst and functional-analyst subagents. Never grant write access unless you have a very specific and controlled use case.

github._when_to_use: Gives Claude access to GitHub issues, PRs, and code review data. Useful for context during code review sessions or for the functional-analyst to read issue descriptions.
